sanskrit:
कुलक्षये प्रणश्यन्ति कुलधर्माः सनातनाः।
धर्मे नष्टे कुलं कृत्स्नमधर्मोऽभिभवत्युत।।
transliteration:
kula-kṣaye praṇaśyanti kula-dharmāḥ sanātanāḥ
dharme naṣṭe kulaṃ kṛtsnam adharmo ’bhibhavaty uta
literal_meaning: In the destruction of the family, the eternal family duties perish; when duty is lost, the entire family is overwhelmed by unrighteousness.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna voices a terror that the cosmic order itself will collapse if the generational chain of ethical transmission is broken, projecting his private dread of moral contamination onto the social fabric. Psychologically, he equates the death of elders with the death of internalized parental superego, leaving the psyche flooded by instinctual chaos.
psychological_state: A person frozen between loyalty to inherited values and terror that breaking the chain will unleash inner and outer lawlessness.
trigger_scenario: A millennial refuses to join the family business, triggering panic that ancestral traditions will die and younger siblings will lose their moral compass.
anchor_text: Protect the family’s moral core; its collapse lets chaos flood the self.
